message: fix: link vision prose to latest

@@ -98,7 +98,7 @@ Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex revi
 Close as `clawhub`: the request is coherent and useful, but current `main` positions this kind of workspace-file integrity monitoring as extension/plugin territory rather than OpenClaw core.
 
 What I checked:
-- Vision scopes optional capability to plugins: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52) says core should stay lean and that optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision scopes optional capability to plugins: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core should stay lean and that optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Hooks already cover bootstrap and startup lifecycle: Internal hooks already expose `agent:bootstrap` and `gateway:startup`, which are the exact lifecycle points the issue asks for when hashing persona/bootstrap files at startup and during agent bootstrap. ([`docs/automation/hooks.md:34`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/automation/hooks.md#L34), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Hook events can emit user-visible alerts: Hook handlers can push strings onto `event.messages`, and the internal hook event type defines `messages` specifically as messages sent back to the user. That matches the issue's proposed 'my brain was just replaced' alert behavior. ([`src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts:3`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts#L3), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Bootstrap hooks receive the workspace bootstrap file set: `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` passes `workspaceDir` and mutable `bootstrapFiles` through the `agent:bootstrap` event, so a hook/plugin can inspect or hash [`AGENTS.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/AGENTS.md)/`SOUL.md`/`USER.md`-class files without core changes. ([`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts:7`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts#L7), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))

@@ -97,8 +97,8 @@ Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex revi
 The request is a real idea, but current `main` treats this as community-plugin territory rather than an OpenClaw core cleanup item: new channel integrations are documented as plugins, community/ClawHub is the canonical distribution path, and the docs already point to an external Vapi bridge.
 
 What I checked:
-- Vision keeps optional integrations out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably hosted and maintained outside this repo, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
-- Vision scope guardrails match this request: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L101) guardrails explicitly list commercial service integrations and wrapper channels as things OpenClaw will not merge for now unless there is unusually strong rationale. ([`VISION.md:101`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L101), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision keeps optional integrations out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably hosted and maintained outside this repo, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision scope guardrails match this request: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) guardrails explicitly list commercial service integrations and wrapper channels as things OpenClaw will not merge for now unless there is unusually strong rationale. ([`VISION.md:101`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L101), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - New channels are expected to be plugins: The channel-plugin guide says a new messaging platform should be built as a ChannelPlugin, with core keeping the shared message tool while the plugin owns config, security, pairing, outbound delivery, and threading. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md#L11), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Community/ClawHub is the canonical path: Current docs say community plugins can extend OpenClaw with channels and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. ([`docs/plugins/community.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/community.md#L9), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Repo already points to an external Vapi bridge: The showcase on current main includes 'Clawdia phone bridge', described as a 'Vapi voice assistant to OpenClaw HTTP bridge' for near real-time phone calls. ([`docs/start/showcase.md:282`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/start/showcase.md#L282), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))

@@ -94,7 +94,7 @@ Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 Current main already provides the requested integration path: OpenClaw documents generic OpenAI-compatible/custom-provider setup, documents provider plugins as the extension surface for new proxies/providers, and includes a concrete community proxy integration example. The proxy behavior is also covered by current tests.
 
 What I checked:
-- Vision keeps optional integrations out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md) says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the preferred path is external plugin distribution rather than growing core support. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision keeps optional integrations out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the preferred path is external plugin distribution rather than growing core support. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Provider-plugin docs explicitly cover OpenAI-compatible proxies: Current docs say this guide is for cases where you want to add an OpenAI-compatible proxy or custom LLM to OpenClaw. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:5`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f42/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L5), [`1daa552d5f42`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f42))
 - Generic custom-provider config is documented on main: [`docs/concepts/model-providers.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f42/docs/concepts/model-providers.md) includes a `models.providers.<id>` example for local OpenAI-compatible proxies and notes the runtime behavior for non-native proxy endpoints. ([`docs/concepts/model-providers.md:566`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f42/docs/concepts/model-providers.md#L566), [`1daa552d5f42`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f42))
 - Main now has a first-party community-proxy guide: [`docs/providers/claude-max-api-proxy.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f42/docs/providers/claude-max-api-proxy.md) documents a community cost-saving proxy and shows OpenClaw configuration via `OPENAI_BASE_URL` to a custom `/v1` endpoint. ([`docs/providers/claude-max-api-proxy.md:78`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f42/docs/providers/claude-max-api-proxy.md#L78), [`1daa552d5f42`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f42))

@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@ Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex revi
 Close as `clawhub`: current `main` already provides a dedicated external memory-plugin path, and project policy says optional memory backends should ship as community plugins rather than new bundled core integrations.
 
 What I checked:
-- Vision: optional capability should stay plugin-side: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md) says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, plugin authors should host and maintain plugins in their own repositories, and memory is a dedicated plugin slot. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision: optional capability should stay plugin-side: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, plugin authors should host and maintain plugins in their own repositories, and memory is a dedicated plugin slot. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Plugin guide: do not add plugins to this repo: [`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md) explicitly says plugin authors do not need to add their plugin to the OpenClaw repository and should publish to ClawHub or npm for `openclaw plugins install`. ([`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L16), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Manifest contract: memory is an exclusive plugin kind: [`docs/plugins/manifest.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/manifest.md) documents `kind: "memory"` as an exclusive plugin kind selected through `plugins.slots.memory`. ([`docs/plugins/manifest.md:676`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/manifest.md#L676), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Public API: third-party memory plugins are supported: [`src/plugins/types.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/plugins/types.ts) exposes `registerMemoryCapability`, `registerMemoryRuntime`, and `registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider` on the public plugin API specifically for memory plugins. ([`src/plugins/types.ts:2024`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/plugins/types.ts#L2024), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))

@@ -95,8 +95,8 @@ Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex revi
 Current main points this proposal toward an external ClawHub/plugin companion, not OpenClaw core: channel-specific runtime belongs outside core, ClawHub is the intended distribution path, and the built-in docs already cover the core hardening knobs for risky/shared channel ingress.
 
 What I checked:
-- Vision: optional capability stays out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L54) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably maintained in the builder's own repository, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L54), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
-- Vision: wrapper-channel guardrail: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L104) explicitly says wrapper channels around already supported channels are not in scope unless there is a clear capability or security gap. ([`VISION.md:104`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L104), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
+- Vision: optional capability stays out of core: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably maintained in the builder's own repository, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L54), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
+- Vision: wrapper-channel guardrail: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) explicitly says wrapper channels around already supported channels are not in scope unless there is a clear capability or security gap. ([`VISION.md:104`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/VISION.md#L104), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
 - ClawHub is the plugin distribution path: The plugin CLI docs show ClawHub-first installation and an explicit `clawhub:` locator, which is the repo's normal path for shipping optional plugin functionality. ([`docs/cli/plugins.md:57`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/docs/cli/plugins.md#L57), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
 - Current security guidance already covers shared-ingress hardening: The security docs recommend `session.dmScope="per-channel-peer"` / `per-account-channel-peer` for shared inboxes and say mutually untrusted operators should be separated with different gateways or OS users/hosts. ([`docs/cli/security.md:28`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/docs/cli/security.md#L28), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))
 - WhatsApp is already plugin-scoped and recommends separate numbers: Current docs treat WhatsApp as an install-on-demand plugin package (`@openclaw/whatsapp`) and recommend running it on a separate number when possible. ([`docs/channels/whatsapp.md:12`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a86/docs/channels/whatsapp.md#L12), [`d4d307e07a86`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a86))

@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@ What I checked:
 - Plugin API already supports additive system-prompt text: `before_prompt_build` can return `prependSystemContext` and `appendSystemContext`, explicitly intended for static plugin guidance appended to the agent system prompt. ([`src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts:28`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts#L28), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Prompt hooks are agent-aware and applied onto the assembled prompt: Hook context includes `agentId`, and the runtime passes that context into prompt-build hooks before composing `appendSystemContext` onto the base system prompt. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1943`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1943), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - Prompt mutation is the documented plugin path: Plugin architecture guidance says to prefer `before_prompt_build` for prompt mutation work. ([`docs/plugins/architecture.md:100`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/architecture.md#L100), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
-- Project scope points optional capability to plugins / ClawHub: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and community plugin docs say third-party capability should be published on ClawHub. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Project scope points optional capability to plugins / ClawHub: [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and community plugin docs say third-party capability should be published on ClawHub. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 
 So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
